question:
Let $\{x\}$ denote the distance between the real number $x$ and the
nearest integer.  For each positive integer $n$, evaluate
\[F_n=\sum_{m=1}^{6n-1} \min(\{\frac{m}{6n}\},\{\frac{m}{3n}\}).\]
(Here $\min(a,b)$ denotes the minimum of $a$ and $b$.)
solution:
It is trivial to check that $\frac{m}{6n}=\{\frac{m}{6n}\}\leq
\{\frac{m}{3n}\}$ for $1\leq m\leq 2n$, that
$1-\frac{m}{3n}=\{\frac{m}{3n}\}\leq \{\frac{m}{6n}\}$ for $2n\leq
m\leq 3n$, that $\frac{m}{3n}-1=\{\frac{m}{3n}\}\leq \{\frac{m}{6n}\}$
for $3n\leq m\leq 4n$, and that $1-\frac{m}{6n}=\{\frac{m}{6n}\}\leq
\{\frac{m}{3n}\}$ for $4n\leq m\leq 6n$.  Therefore the desired sum is
\begin{gather*}
\sum_{m=1}^{2n-1} \frac{m}{6n}
 +\sum_{m=2n}^{3n-1} \left(1-\frac{m}{3n} \right) \\
 +\sum_{m=3n}^{4n-1} \left(\frac{m}{3n}-1 \right) + \sum_{m=4n}^{6n-1} \left(
1-\frac{m}{6n} \right)
=n.
\end{gather*}

descriptive_long.question:
Let $\{realvar\}$ denote the distance between the real number $realvar$ and the
nearest integer.  For each positive integer $posintn$, evaluate
\[sumvalue=\sum_{indexm=1}^{6posintn-1} \min(\{\frac{indexm}{6posintn}\},\{\frac{indexm}{3posintn}\}).\]
(Here $\min(a,b)$ denotes the minimum of $a$ and $b$.)
descriptive_long.solution:
It is trivial to check that $\frac{indexm}{6posintn}=\{\frac{indexm}{6posintn}\}\leq
\{\frac{indexm}{3posintn}\}$ for $1\leq indexm\leq 2posintn$, that
$1-\frac{indexm}{3posintn}=\{\frac{indexm}{3posintn}\}\leq \{\frac{indexm}{6posintn}\}$ for $2posintn\leq
indexm\leq 3posintn$, that $\frac{indexm}{3posintn}-1=\{\frac{indexm}{3posintn}\}\leq \{\frac{indexm}{6posintn}\}$
for $3posintn\leq indexm\leq 4posintn$, and that $1-\frac{indexm}{6posintn}=\{\frac{indexm}{6posintn}\}\leq
\{\frac{indexm}{3posintn}\}$ for $4posintn\leq indexm\leq 6posintn$.  Therefore the desired sum is
\begin{gather*}
\sum_{indexm=1}^{2posintn-1} \frac{indexm}{6posintn}
 +\sum_{indexm=2posintn}^{3posintn-1} \left(1-\frac{indexm}{3posintn} \right) \\
 +\sum_{indexm=3posintn}^{4posintn-1} \left(\frac{indexm}{3posintn}-1 \right) + \sum_{indexm=4posintn}^{6posintn-1} \left(
1-\frac{indexm}{6posintn} \right)
=posintn.
\end{gather*}

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Let $\{waterfall\}$ denote the distance between the real number $waterfall$ and the
nearest integer.  For each positive integer $avalanche$, evaluate
\[puzzlement=\sum_{sandpaper=1}^{6avalanche-1} \min(\{\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}\},\{\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}\}).\]
(Here $\min(a,b)$ denotes the minimum of $a$ and $b$.)
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
It is trivial to check that $\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}=\{\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}\}\leq
\{\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}\}$ for $1\leq sandpaper\leq 2avalanche$, that
$1-\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}=\{\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}\}\leq \{\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}\}$ for $2avalanche\leq
sandpaper\leq 3avalanche$, that $\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}-1=\{\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}\}\leq \{\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}\}$
for $3avalanche\leq sandpaper\leq 4avalanche$, and that $1-\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}=\{\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}\}\leq
\{\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}\}$ for $4avalanche\leq sandpaper\leq 6avalanche$.  Therefore the desired sum is
\begin{gather*}
\sum_{sandpaper=1}^{2avalanche-1} \frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche}
 +\sum_{sandpaper=2avalanche}^{3avalanche-1} \left(1-\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche} \right) \\
 +\sum_{sandpaper=3avalanche}^{4avalanche-1} \left(\frac{sandpaper}{3avalanche}-1 \right) + \sum_{sandpaper=4avalanche}^{6avalanche-1} \left(
1-\frac{sandpaper}{6avalanche} \right)
=avalanche.
\end{gather*}

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Let $\{imaginaryval\}$ denote the distance between the real number $imaginaryval$ and the
nearest integer.  For each positive integer $forevercount$, evaluate
\[gapfunction=\sum_{fixedindex=1}^{6forevercount-1} \min(\{\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}\},\{\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}\}).\]
(Here $\min(a,b)$ denotes the minimum of $a$ and $b$.)
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
It is trivial to check that $\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}=\{\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}\}\leq
\{\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}\}$ for $1\leq fixedindex\leq 2forevercount$, that
$1-\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}=\{\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}\}\leq \{\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}\}$ for $2forevercount\leq
fixedindex\leq 3forevercount$, that $\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}-1=\{\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}\}\leq \{\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}\}$
for $3forevercount\leq fixedindex\leq 4forevercount$, and that $1-\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}=\{\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}\}\leq
\{\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}\}$ for $4forevercount\leq fixedindex\leq 6forevercount$.  Therefore the desired sum is
\begin{gather*}
\sum_{fixedindex=1}^{2forevercount-1} \frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount}
 +\sum_{fixedindex=2forevercount}^{3forevercount-1} \left(1-\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount} \right) \\
 +\sum_{fixedindex=3forevercount}^{4forevercount-1} \left(\frac{fixedindex}{3forevercount}-1 \right) + \sum_{fixedindex=4forevercount}^{6forevercount-1} \left(
1-\frac{fixedindex}{6forevercount} \right)
=forevercount.
\end{gather*}

garbled_string.question:
Let $\{qzxwvtnp\}$ denote the distance between the real number $qzxwvtnp$ and the
nearest integer.  For each positive integer $bfkpqzow$, evaluate
\[
klqprjsn=\sum_{hjgrksla=1}^{6bfkpqzow-1} \min(\{\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}\},\{\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}\}).
\]
(Here $\min(a,b)$ denotes the minimum of $a$ and $b$.)
garbled_string.solution:
It is trivial to check that $\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}=\{\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}\}\leq
\{\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}\}$ for $1\leq hjgrksla\leq 2bfkpqzow$, that
$1-\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}=\{\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}\}\leq \{\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}\}$ for $2bfkpqzow\leq
hjgrksla\leq 3bfkpqzow$, that $\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}-1=\{\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}\}\leq \{\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}\}$
for $3bfkpqzow\leq hjgrksla\leq 4bfkpqzow$, and that $1-\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}=\{\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}\}\leq
\{\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}\}$ for $4bfkpqzow\leq hjgrksla\leq 6bfkpqzow$.  Therefore the desired sum is
\begin{gather*}
\sum_{hjgrksla=1}^{2bfkpqzow-1} \frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow}
 +\sum_{hjgrksla=2bfkpqzow}^{3bfkpqzow-1} \left(1-\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow} \right) \\
 +\sum_{hjgrksla=3bfkpqzow}^{4bfkpqzow-1} \left(\frac{hjgrksla}{3bfkpqzow}-1 \right) + \sum_{hjgrksla=4bfkpqzow}^{6bfkpqzow-1} \left(
1-\frac{hjgrksla}{6bfkpqzow} \right)
=bfkpqzow.
\end{gather*}

kernel_variant.question:
Let $\{x\}$ denote the distance between the real number $x$ and the nearest integer.  For every positive integer $n$ evaluate
\[
G_n\;=\;\sum_{m=1}^{12n-1}\min\Bigl(\Bigl\{\frac{m}{12n}\Bigr\},\Bigl\{\frac{m}{6n}\Bigr\}\Bigr).
\]
kernel_variant.solution:
Write
\[
A_m=\bigl\{\tfrac{m}{12n}\bigr\},\qquad B_m=\bigl\{\tfrac{m}{6n}\bigr\}\quad(1\le m\le12n-1).
\]
For a real number $t$, $\{t\}=t$ if $t\le\tfrac12$ and $\{t\}=1-t$ if $t\ge\tfrac12$.  Using the facts that $A_m\le\tfrac12$ for $m\le6n$ and $B_m\le\tfrac12$ for $m\le3n$ or $6n\le m\le9n$, the behaviour of $A_m$ and $B_m$---and hence of $\min(A_m,B_m)$---is completely linear on the following five blocks of indices.

1. $1\le m\le3n-1$:  $A_m=\dfrac m{12n},\;B_m=\dfrac m{6n}$, so $\min(A_m,B_m)=A_m=\dfrac m{12n}$.

2. $3n\le m\le4n-1$:  $A_m=\dfrac m{12n},\;B_m=1-\dfrac m{6n}$, and one checks $A_m\le B_m$, hence the minimum is $A_m$.

3. $4n\le m\le6n-1$:  $A_m=\dfrac m{12n},\;B_m=1-\dfrac m{6n}$ with $B_m\le A_m$, so the minimum is $B_m=1-\dfrac m{6n}$.

4. $6n\le m\le8n-1$:  $A_m=1-\dfrac m{12n},\;B_m=\dfrac{m-6n}{6n}$ and now $B_m\le A_m$, so the minimum is $B_m=\dfrac{m-6n}{6n}$.

5. $8n\le m\le12n-1$:  $A_m=1-\dfrac m{12n},\;B_m$ equals either $\dfrac{m-6n}{6n}$ or $1-\dfrac{m-6n}{6n}$, but in either subcase $A_m\le B_m$, hence the minimum is $A_m=1-\dfrac m{12n}$.

Breaking the sum into these five arithmetic pieces we compute:
\[
\begin{aligned}
\sum_{m=1}^{3n-1}\frac m{12n}&=\frac{(3n)(3n-1)}{24n}=\frac{3n-1}8,\\
\sum_{m=3n}^{4n-1}\frac m{12n}&=\frac1{12n}\cdot\frac{n(7n-1)}2=\frac{7n-1}{24},\\
\sum_{m=4n}^{6n-1}\Bigl(1-\frac m{6n}\Bigr)&=2n-\frac1{6n}\,n(10n-1)=\frac{2n+1}6,\\
\sum_{m=6n}^{8n-1}\frac{m-6n}{6n}&=\frac{(2n-1)n}{6n}=\frac{2n-1}6,\\
\sum_{m=8n}^{12n-1}\Bigl(1-\frac m{12n}\Bigr)&=4n-\frac1{12n}\,2n(20n-1)=\frac{4n+1}6.
\end{aligned}
\]
Adding these five results gives
\[
\frac{3n-1}8+\frac{7n-1}{24}+\frac{2n+1}6+\frac{2n-1}6+\frac{4n+1}6=2n.
\]
Hence for every positive integer $n$ one has
\[G_n=2n.\]
\boxed{2n}

core_steps: Partition the index set by solving {m/a n} ≤ {m/b n} and the reverse; this yields finitely many subintervals with simple linear behavior.
On each subinterval, write min({m/a n},{m/b n}) as either m/(a n) or 1−m/(b n) (or m/(b n)−1, etc.), i.e. an affine function of m.
Compute the arithmetic sums of those affine functions over each subinterval.
Add the sub-sums; all linear and quadratic terms cancel, leaving a constant multiple of n.

slot_den_large.description: larger denominator appearing in the fractional parts (currently 6 in {m/6n})

slot_den_small.description: smaller denominator (currently 3 in {m/3n}); must divide slot_den_large and yield exactly two times smaller in the official data

slot_upper_limit.description: upper index of the sum, presently (slot_den_large)·n−1

slot_boundaries.description: the values where the two fractional parts are equal, currently multiples 2n, 3n, 4n that split the range; in general they are (slot_den_large/slot_den_small−1)·n, slot_den_small·n, (slot_den_large/slot_den_small+1)·n, etc.
